whatWasImplemented: src/app/api/files/[id]/route.ts — DELETE handler. 401 without auth. Lookup the file via the user-session client so RLS filters cross-user ids to null (-> 404). Upgrade to service-role for the actual cascade: Storage remove -> embeddings delete WHERE file_id -> supplementary_files row delete -> usage_counters.files_stored -= 1 (clamped at 0).
Ownership check piggybacks on the existing SELECT RLS policy from M5F1 — no second check needed; if the row isn't visible to the user, it doesn't exist from their perspective.
Storage.remove errors are logged but non-blocking: the DB cleanup proceeds even if the object is already missing (orphaned from a prior failed upload).
src/__tests__/file-delete.test.ts — 5 tests: 401 no auth, 404 cross-user, happy-path deletes Storage + embeddings + row + decrements counter, zero-counter guard, storage-error best-effort completion.
whatWasLeftUndone: Integration test against real Storage object + real DB deferred; the mock-level coverage asserts every step of the cascade fires with the expected shape.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 423 passed / 1 skipped / 56 files (+5 new at M5F4 layer).
